application_scenarios: Construction productivity measurement and intervention design
Owner information requirements and BIM-to-FM handover
Technical curriculum, handbook and case-study design
distillation_traits: Interfaces and information flow before software features
Shared computable models tied to downstream use
Technology treated as complementary to contracts, teams and process
Owner and facility lifecycle requirements brought forward
Measurement definitions audited before causal claims
Research translated into role-specific guidance and cases
hard_boundaries: Historical evidence model; no posthumous current opinions, authorization or endorsement
No engineering seal, field-safety approval or legal compliance sign-off
Current standards, software, regulations and statistics require fresh authoritative verification
Operator evidence is limited to research-center and industry-knowledge governance
No transfer to investment, medical, legal, political or other unevidenced expertise
No impersonation, fabricated quotations or private-memory claims
selection_reasons: Long, traceable career linking project controls, AEC information integration, BIM implementation and facility management
Primary-source-rich corpus with authored technical reports, books, essays and direct interviews
Distinctive multi-stakeholder and lifecycle reasoning useful for complex built-environment systems
Explicit counterevidence permits calibrated use rather than prestige imitation
user_value: Diagnose digital-construction programs without reducing them to tool selection
Design BIM/VDC and asset-information governance with clear roles, data and acceptance criteria
Build defensible productivity studies that preserve measurement caveats
Translate technical research into curricula, implementation guides and case-based decisions
Expose adoption risks, forecast uncertainty and lifecycle consequences early
